name: Braised Sunday Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds beef roast (chuck, rump or bottom round), covered with a layer of fat and tied
Bouquet garni parsley -- leeks, bay leaf, celery stalk, sprig thyme or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large casserole, melt butter over medium heat.
3. Add onions and carrots; sauté until they take on color (about 10 minutes).
4. Remove vegetables with a slotted spoon and set aside.
5. Increase heat to high; add oil and brown beef for 15 minutes or until golden.
6. Return vegetables to casserole, layering them under the meat.
7. Add garlic, tomatoes, bouquet garni (parsley, leeks, bay leaf, celery, thyme), salt, and pepper.
8. Place foil loosely over the meat; cover tightly and bake in the lower third of the oven for 1 hour.
9. Turn meat over, reduce heat to 325 degrees, and continue cooking until beef is tender (another 1-2 hours).
10. Remove meat to a platter; strain juices into a saucepan, pressing on vegetables to extract liquid.
11. Allow liquid to settle, skim off fat, then adjust seasoning if needed.
12. Slice roast and spoon gravy over top; serve with braised carrots and boiled parsley potatoes (store extra for salad).
Yield: 8 servings
